![Marmidon company logo](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

**MARMIDON ENTERPRISE SOFTWARE · WEBSITE PROGRAMME**

**MARM-WEB-TSR-004**

**Technical, SEO & Analytics Specification**

Build Stack, Performance, Security, Search & Measurement

_The engineering contract for <www.marmidon.com> - the technology stack, hosting, performance budgets, security hardening, search-engine optimisation, structured data, analytics and the conversion-measurement framework the developer must implement._

| **Document**   | Technical, SEO & Analytics Specification (TSR)  |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| **Programme**  | Marmidon Corporate Website Revamp 2026          |
| **Parent**     | MARM-WEB-PRD-001 (Requirements)                 |
| **Companions** | WDD-002, SIA-003, CPM-005                       |
| **Version**    | 1.0 - Issued for Development                    |
| **Targets**    | LCP ≤2.5s · INP ≤200ms · CLS ≤0.1 · WCAG 2.2 AA |
| **Audience**   | Web developer, DevOps, SEO & analytics          |

**PREPARED BY**

**Marmidon Website Programme - Business Analysis Workstream**

Senior Business Analyst (Corporate & Enterprise Web)

_CONFIDENTIAL - For the Marmidon website development team and authorised stakeholders only._

**Table of Contents**

**SECTION 1**

**Purpose & Scope**

What this specification governs

## **1.1 Purpose**

_This document is the technical source of truth for the Marmidon website build. It converts the requirements in MARM-WEB-PRD-001 and the design in MARM-WEB-WDD-002 into concrete engineering, search and measurement instructions._

Where the PRD states what the site must do and the WDD states how it must look, this specification states how it must be built, served, secured, found and measured. Every non-functional requirement (NFR-01 to NFR-10) in the PRD is given a measurable, testable implementation here.

**MUST-HAVE · Authority of this document**

For all technical, SEO, analytics, security and performance matters, this specification governs.

Where a conflict appears between documents, the order of precedence is: PRD-001 (scope) → TSR-004 (technical) → WDD-002 (visual) → CPM-005 (content).

Any deviation must be raised with the Marmidon programme lead and recorded in the change log before implementation.

## **1.2 In Scope**

| **Area**      | **What this document specifies**                                          |
| ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Stack & CMS   | Recommended build approach, CMS, rendering model, component framework     |
| Hosting       | Infrastructure, CDN, TLS, DNS, environments, backups                      |
| Performance   | Core Web Vitals budgets, asset budgets, caching, image strategy           |
| Security      | HTTPS, headers, OWASP mitigations, form & bot hardening                   |
| SEO           | Metadata, canonical, structured data, XML sitemap, robots, i18n-readiness |
| Analytics     | Tag manager, GA4, consent-gated events, conversion taxonomy               |
| Integrations  | Form→CRM/email, CAPTCHA, cookie consent, calendar/booking                 |
| Compatibility | Browser & device support matrix, graceful degradation                     |
| Accessibility | WCAG 2.2 AA technical conformance checklist                               |
| Compliance    | Privacy, cookies, data retention, legal pages                             |
| Delivery      | Environments, CI/CD, QA gates, launch & rollback                          |

## **1.3 Out of Scope**

- Visual design tokens, component appearance and wireframes - owned by WDD-002.
- Page-level copy and the content matrix - owned by CPM-005.
- The Marmidon ERP product itself, its licensing and its in-product analytics.
- Paid-media campaign configuration (the site must be ready to receive it; campaign setup is a marketing activity).

**SECTION 2**

**Technology Stack & CMS**

How the site should be built

## **2.1 Architecture Principle**

_A content-led corporate site with a managed, structured CMS, server-rendered or statically-generated HTML for speed and SEO, and a component-based front end aligned to the WDD component library._

**BENCHMARK · What the benchmarked vendors do**

NetSuite, SAP and Microsoft run large enterprise DXP/CMS platforms; Odoo, Acumatica, Zoho and SYSPRO run leaner structured CMS stacks.

Common to all: server-rendered marketing pages, a structured content model (reusable module/sector/case entries), a global component library, and a hard separation between content authoring and presentation.

Marmidon should follow the leaner pattern - a structured CMS plus a fast front end - not a heavyweight DXP it does not need.

## **2.2 Recommended Approach**

| **Layer** | **Recommendation**                                                              | **Rationale**                                                            |
| --------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Rendering | Server-side rendering (SSR) or static generation (SSG) with incremental updates | Fast first paint, fully crawlable HTML, strong Core Web Vitals           |
| CMS       | Headless or hybrid CMS with a structured content model                          | Reusable module/sector/case entries; non-technical authoring per CPM-005 |
| Front end | Component-based framework matching the WDD library                              | One component set, consistent UI, maintainable                           |
| Styling   | Design-token-driven CSS (tokens from WDD §3)                                    | Single source of brand truth; theme changes in one place                 |
| Forms     | Progressive-enhanced HTML forms posting to a server endpoint                    | Work without JS; integrate to CRM/email server-side                      |
| Search    | Built-in site search over the structured content index                          | Supports SIA-003 search requirement                                      |

**DEVELOPER GUIDANCE · Developer latitude**

Marmidon is not prescribing a single named product. Any stack that satisfies these properties is acceptable: structured CMS, SSR/SSG HTML, token-driven components, server-side form handling.

The developer must record the chosen stack, versions and hosting in a short Solution Architecture note and submit it before build sign-off.

## **2.3 Content Model (CMS Entry Types)**

The CMS must model the following reusable entry types so that content is structured, not free HTML. This directly supports the page inventory in SIA-003 and the content matrix in CPM-005.

| **Entry type**   | **Key fields**                                                             | **Used by**                 |
| ---------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------- |
| Module           | Name, slug, summary, hero, feature blocks, benefits, related sectors, CTA  | 11 module pages             |
| Sector           | Name, slug, intro, pain points, recommended modules, proof, CTA            | 12 sector pages             |
| Case study       | Client, sector, modules used, challenge, solution, outcomes/metrics, quote | Customers hub & detail      |
| Resource/Article | Title, slug, type, author, date, body, tags, related                       | Blog, guides, resources hub |
| Person           | Name, role, photo, bio                                                     | About / leadership          |
| Navigation       | Label, URL, parent, order                                                  | Header, mega-menu, footer   |
| Global           | Logo, contact, social, legal links, default SEO                            | Site-wide                   |

**SECTION 3**

**Hosting, Infrastructure & CDN**

How the site is served

## **3.1 Hosting Requirements**

| **Requirement** | **Specification**                                                     |
| --------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Uptime          | ≥ 99.9% monthly availability (NFR-04)                                 |
| TLS             | HTTPS everywhere; TLS 1.2 minimum, TLS 1.3 preferred; HTTP→HTTPS 301  |
| CDN             | Global CDN in front of all static assets and HTML where cacheable     |
| HTTP            | HTTP/2 minimum; HTTP/3 where supported by the CDN                     |
| Compression     | Brotli (preferred) or gzip on all text assets                         |
| Region          | Primary edge coverage for East Africa, EU and global visitors         |
| Backups         | Automated daily backups of CMS content and database; 30-day retention |
| DNS             | Managed DNS; <www.marmidon.com> canonical host; apex → www redirect   |

## **3.2 Domain & DNS**

The canonical production host is <www.marmidon.com>. The apex domain marmidon.com must 301-redirect to the www host (or vice versa - one canonical host, chosen and enforced consistently). The placeholder host marmidon.kimdigitary.com is decommissioned after the redirect map in SIA-003 §6 is live and verified.

**MUST-HAVE · TLS & HSTS**

Valid certificate covering www and apex; auto-renewal configured.

Strict-Transport-Security header with a long max-age once HTTPS is verified stable.

No mixed content: every asset, script and font loads over HTTPS.

## **3.3 Environments**

| **Environment** | **Purpose**                      | **Access**                                                 |
| --------------- | -------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| Local           | Developer machines               | Developer only                                             |
| Staging         | Client review, QA, UAT (PRD §12) | Marmidon team + developer; noindex; password/IP-restricted |
| Production      | Live <www.marmidon.com>          | Public; full indexing, analytics & monitoring              |

**MUST-HAVE · Staging must never be indexed**

Staging must return noindex and be blocked from crawlers, and must not carry production analytics.

A staging site leaking into Google is a real SEO incident - enforce noindex + robots + access control on every non-production environment.

**SECTION 4**

**Performance Engineering**

Core Web Vitals & asset budgets

## **4.1 Core Web Vitals Targets**

These are pass/fail acceptance targets, measured at the 75th percentile on mobile, in line with PRD NFR-01.

| **≤2.5s**<br><br>**LCP** | **≤200ms**<br><br>**INP** | **≤0.1**<br><br>**CLS** | **≤1.8s**<br><br>**FCP target** |
| ------------------------ | ------------------------- | ----------------------- | ------------------------------- |

| **Metric**                      | **Target (good)** | **Hard ceiling** | **Primary levers**                        |
| ------------------------------- | ----------------- | ---------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| LCP - Largest Contentful Paint  | **≤ 2.5s**        | 4.0s             | Optimised hero image, preload, SSR, CDN   |
| INP - Interaction to Next Paint | **≤ 200ms**       | 500ms            | Minimal JS, code-split, avoid long tasks  |
| CLS - Cumulative Layout Shift   | **≤ 0.1**         | 0.25             | Sized media, reserved space, font-display |
| FCP - First Contentful Paint    | **≤ 1.8s**        | 3.0s             | Critical CSS, fast TTFB, CDN              |
| TTFB - Time to First Byte       | **≤ 0.8s**        | 1.8s             | Caching, edge rendering, fast host        |

## **4.2 Asset Budgets (per page, mobile)**

| **Asset class**               | **Budget**              | **Notes**                            |
| ----------------------------- | ----------------------- | ------------------------------------ |
| Total transfer (initial view) | ≤ 1.4 MB                | Compressed; lazy-load below the fold |
| JavaScript (parsed)           | ≤ 300 KB                | Code-split; defer non-critical       |
| CSS                           | ≤ 120 KB                | Token-driven; purge unused           |
| Hero image                    | ≤ 200 KB                | AVIF/WebP, responsive srcset         |
| Web fonts                     | ≤ 2 families, ≤ 4 files | WOFF2, font-display: swap, preload   |
| Requests (initial)            | ≤ 50                    | Bundle & cache aggressively          |

## **4.3 Image & Media Strategy**

- Serve next-gen formats (AVIF or WebP) with appropriate fallbacks.
- Responsive images via srcset/sizes; never ship a desktop hero to a phone.
- Explicit width and height (or aspect-ratio) on every image to prevent layout shift (CLS).
- Lazy-load all below-the-fold imagery; eager-load the LCP hero only.
- Compress and strip metadata at build time; no uncompressed originals in production.

## **4.4 Caching & Delivery**

| **Layer**                       | **Policy**                                                               |
| ------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Static assets (JS/CSS/img/font) | Long cache (1 year) with content-hashed filenames; immutable             |
| HTML pages                      | Short cache or CDN edge cache with revalidation; instant content updates |
| CMS API/content                 | Cached at build (SSG) or short TTL (SSR) with purge-on-publish           |
| Fonts                           | Preload, self-host where licensing allows, long cache                    |

**BEST PRACTICE · Performance is a launch gate**

A Lighthouse / CrUX check is part of the acceptance gates in PRD §12.

The site must pass the Core Web Vitals targets on the homepage, a module page and a sector page before sign-off.

**SECTION 5**

**Security & Hardening**

Protecting the site and its visitors

## **5.1 Transport & Headers**

| **Header / control**              | **Requirement**                                                            |
| --------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| HTTPS / TLS                       | All traffic over TLS 1.2+; HTTP redirects to HTTPS                         |
| HSTS                              | Strict-Transport-Security with long max-age once stable                    |
| Content-Security-Policy           | Restrict scripts/styles/frames to known origins; no inline where avoidable |
| X-Content-Type-Options            | nosniff                                                                    |
| X-Frame-Options / frame-ancestors | Deny framing except where explicitly required                              |
| Referrer-Policy                   | strict-origin-when-cross-origin                                            |
| Permissions-Policy                | Disable unused browser features (camera, mic, geolocation)                 |

## **5.2 OWASP Top-10 Mitigations**

| **Risk**                       | **Mitigation on this site**                                      |
| ------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Injection                      | Parameterised queries; never build queries/markup from raw input |
| Broken access control          | CMS admin behind authentication; least-privilege roles           |
| Security misconfiguration      | Hardened headers; no directory listing; no stack traces in prod  |
| Vulnerable components          | Pinned, patched dependencies; regular update cadence             |
| Identification & auth failures | Strong CMS auth, lockout, optional MFA for admins                |
| SSRF / server abuse            | Validate and allow-list any server-side fetches                  |
| Logging & monitoring           | Centralised logs; alert on anomalies and 5xx spikes              |

## **5.3 Form & Bot Hardening**

- Server-side validation of every field; never trust client-side validation alone.
- Spam/bot protection on all public forms (CAPTCHA or invisible challenge - see §8).
- Rate-limiting and honeypot fields on contact/demo forms.
- Email and CRM submission happen server-side; no credentials exposed to the browser.
- Sanitise and escape all user-supplied content before storage or display.

**MUST-HAVE · Demo & contact forms carry the business**

Per the PRD, demo requests are the primary conversion. If these forms break or get buried in spam, the site fails its core job.

Forms must be reliable, validated, protected, logged and delivered to both CRM and a monitored inbox - with a tested fallback if the CRM is unreachable.

**SECTION 6**

**Search Engine Optimisation**

Being found, by people and AI

## **6.1 Crawlability & Indexing**

| **Control**    | **Requirement**                                                   |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Clean HTML     | Fully server-rendered, crawlable content - not JS-only rendering  |
| robots.txt     | Allow production crawl; disallow staging/utility; link to sitemap |
| XML sitemap    | Auto-generated, kept current, submitted to search consoles        |
| Canonical tags | Every page declares a self-referencing canonical URL              |
| URL structure  | Lowercase, hyphenated, /section/page per SIA-003 §5               |
| Redirects      | 301 map from SIA-003 §6 implemented at cutover; no broken links   |
| Pagination     | Crawlable, canonicalised resource/blog listings                   |

## **6.2 On-Page Metadata (per page)**

Every page must expose the following, populated from the CMS and specified per-page in CPM-005.

| **Element**       | **Rule**                                                   |
| ----------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| &lt;title&gt;     | Unique, ≤ 60 chars, primary keyword + brand                |
| Meta description  | Unique, 140-160 chars, benefit-led, includes a call to act |
| Single H1         | Exactly one H1 per page, matching page intent              |
| Heading hierarchy | Logical H1→H2→H3; no skipped levels                        |
| Open Graph        | og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url, og:type        |
| Twitter Card      | summary_large_image with branded share image               |
| Image alt text    | Descriptive alt on every meaningful image                  |

## **6.3 Structured Data (Schema.org / JSON-LD)**

Structured data makes Marmidon eligible for rich results and readable by AI answer engines. Implement the following JSON-LD types.

| **Schema type**                 | **Where**            | **Purpose**                                      |
| ------------------------------- | -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| Organization                    | Site-wide            | Brand, logo, social profiles, contact            |
| WebSite + SearchAction          | Site-wide            | Site identity and search box eligibility         |
| SoftwareApplication / Product   | Module pages         | Describe each ERP module                         |
| BreadcrumbList                  | All deep pages       | Breadcrumb rich results; matches SIA breadcrumbs |
| Article / BlogPosting           | Resources & blog     | Article rich results                             |
| FAQPage                         | Pricing, module FAQs | FAQ rich results where genuine Q&A exists        |
| Review / Quote (as appropriate) | Case studies         | Testimonials and outcomes                        |

**BEST PRACTICE · Built for AI answer engines**

Per PRD NFR and 2026 best practice, content must be readable by AI search and answer engines.

Clean semantic HTML, accurate structured data, clear question-and-answer blocks and descriptive headings all make Marmidon quotable by AI assistants - a growing discovery channel for B2B software.

## **6.4 International & Future-Proofing**

- Set the html lang attribute correctly (en).
- Keep the URL and content model ready for future hreflang/localisation without rework.
- Stable, human-readable slugs that will not need to change (slug changes cost SEO).

**SECTION 7**

**Analytics & Conversion Tracking**

Measuring what matters

## **7.1 Measurement Stack**

| **Component**   | **Recommendation**                                              |
| --------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Tag management  | A tag manager so marketing can manage tags without code deploys |
| Web analytics   | GA4 (or equivalent privacy-respecting analytics)                |
| Consent         | Analytics and marketing tags fire only after consent (see §8)   |
| Search consoles | Verify the property; submit sitemap; monitor coverage           |
| Uptime/RUM      | Uptime monitoring and real-user performance monitoring          |

## **7.2 Conversion Event Taxonomy**

The following events must be implemented consistently. Demo and contact submissions are the primary conversions per the PRD.

| **Event**           | **Trigger**                                | **Type**      |
| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------ | ------------- |
| demo_request_submit | Request-a-Demo form submitted successfully | **PRIMARY**   |
| contact_submit      | Contact form submitted successfully        | **PRIMARY**   |
| lead_form_start     | User begins any lead form                  | Micro         |
| resource_download   | Gated guide/whitepaper downloaded          | **SECONDARY** |
| newsletter_signup   | Newsletter subscription                    | Secondary     |
| module_view         | A module page is viewed                    | Engagement    |
| sector_view         | A sector page is viewed                    | Engagement    |
| case_study_view     | A case study is viewed                     | Engagement    |
| cta_click           | Any primary CTA button click (with label)  | Engagement    |
| pricing_view        | Pricing page viewed                        | Intent        |
| outbound_click      | Click to external/social                   | Engagement    |
| search_query        | Site search performed (with term)          | Engagement    |

**SPECIFICATION · Event naming rules**

snake_case, lowercase, stable names; never rename a live event without versioning.

Attach useful parameters (page path, CTA label, module/sector name, form name) for segmentation.

Mark demo_request_submit and contact_submit as conversions in the analytics platform.

## **7.3 UTM & Campaign Readiness**

- All inbound campaign links use consistent UTM parameters; the analytics layer must capture and attribute them.
- First-touch and last-touch attribution available for demo requests.
- Thank-you/confirmation pages exist for each conversion to anchor goal tracking.

**SECTION 8**

**Integrations & Third-Party Services**

## **8.1 Required Integrations**

| **Integration**             | **Purpose**                         | **Requirement**                                                  |
| --------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| CRM / lead capture          | Route demo & contact leads to sales | Server-side submission; mapped fields; failure fallback to inbox |
| Transactional email         | Notify Marmidon + confirm to user   | Reliable delivery; SPF/DKIM/DMARC aligned                        |
| CAPTCHA / bot defence       | Protect public forms                | Privacy-respecting; accessible alternative                       |
| Cookie consent (CMP)        | Lawful tag firing                   | Blocks non-essential tags until consent                          |
| Maps (contact page)         | Show Marmidon location              | Lazy-loaded; consent-aware if it sets cookies                    |
| Calendar/booking (optional) | Book a demo slot                    | Phase 2; must not block core demo form                           |

## **8.2 Consent & Tag Sequencing**

**MUST-HAVE · No tracking before consent**

Essential cookies only by default. Analytics, marketing and embedded media that set cookies must wait for explicit consent.

The consent banner must offer accept, reject and granular choice, and must record the consent decision.

This is both a legal requirement and a trust signal for enterprise buyers.

**SECTION 9**

**Browser & Device Support**

## **9.1 Support Matrix**

| **Tier**             | **Browsers / devices**                                                | **Standard**                                   |
| -------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| Full support         | Latest 2 versions of Chrome, Edge, Firefox, Safari (desktop & mobile) | Pixel-accurate, fully functional               |
| Functional support   | Older evergreen versions; common Android & iOS devices                | Fully usable; minor visual variance allowed    |
| Graceful degradation | Legacy/edge browsers                                                  | Content accessible; enhancements may be absent |
| Not supported        | Internet Explorer                                                     | No dedicated support                           |

## **9.2 Responsive Breakpoints**

Implement the breakpoints defined in WDD §3 (mobile-first). Verify layouts at small phone, large phone, tablet and desktop widths. Mobile is the primary design target per the PRD.

- Touch targets ≥ 44×44px; no hover-only interactions on touch devices.
- Test on real devices for the homepage, a module page, a sector page and a form.

**SECTION 10**

**Accessibility - WCAG 2.2 AA**

Technical conformance

_The site must meet WCAG 2.2 Level AA (PRD NFR-06). The WDD covers visual accessibility; this section covers the technical conformance the developer must deliver and test._

## **10.1 Conformance Checklist**

| **Area**      | **Requirement**                                                                 |
| ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Semantic HTML | Landmarks (header/nav/main/footer), correct heading order, lists as lists       |
| Keyboard      | Every interactive element reachable & operable by keyboard; logical focus order |
| Focus visible | Clear visible focus indicator on all focusable elements (WCAG 2.2)              |
| Contrast      | Text ≥ 4.5:1 (normal), ≥ 3:1 (large & UI components)                            |
| Alt text      | Meaningful images have alt; decorative images are empty-alt                     |
| Forms         | Programmatic labels, error identification, instructions, no keyboard traps      |
| ARIA          | Used only where native HTML cannot; correct roles & states                      |
| Media         | Captions/transcripts for video; no auto-playing audio                           |
| Motion        | Respect prefers-reduced-motion; no seizure-inducing flashes                     |
| Target size   | Interactive targets meet WCAG 2.2 minimum size guidance                         |
| Zoom/reflow   | Usable at 200% zoom and at 320px width without loss of content                  |
| Language      | Page language set; no content that conveys meaning by colour alone              |

**SPECIFICATION · Accessibility testing is mandatory**

Automated scan (e.g. axe) with zero critical issues on every template.

Manual keyboard-only pass and screen-reader spot-check on key journeys (home, module, demo form).

Accessibility is part of the acceptance gates in PRD §12 - not an optional extra.

**SECTION 11**

**Legal, Privacy & Compliance**

## **11.1 Required Legal Pages**

| **Page**                | **Purpose**                                               |
| ----------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |
| Privacy Policy          | What data is collected, why, how long, and visitor rights |
| Cookie Policy           | Cookies used, categories, and how to change consent       |
| Terms of Use            | Terms governing use of the website                        |
| Accessibility Statement | Conformance level, known issues, contact route            |

## **11.2 Data Handling**

- Collect only what is needed on forms; state the purpose at the point of capture.
- Secure transmission and storage of all lead data; access on a least-privilege basis.
- Defined retention period for lead and analytics data; documented in the privacy policy.
- A clear route for data-subject requests (access, correction, deletion).

**NOTE · Scope note**

This section sets the website's compliance baseline. It is not legal advice. Marmidon should have its privacy, cookie and terms text reviewed by a qualified adviser before launch; the developer implements the agreed text and the consent mechanics.

**SECTION 12**

**Environments, CI/CD, QA & Launch**

## **12.1 Delivery Pipeline**

| **Stage**            | **What happens**                                                       |
| -------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Source control       | All code in version control; trunk + feature branches; reviewed merges |
| Build                | Automated build with asset optimisation and content-hashing            |
| Automated checks     | Lint, accessibility scan, broken-link check, performance budget check  |
| Deploy to staging    | Every change deploys to staging for review (noindex)                   |
| UAT                  | Marmidon signs off against PRD §12 acceptance gates                    |
| Deploy to production | Promoted build; redirects live; monitoring on                          |
| Rollback             | One-step rollback to the previous known-good build                     |

## **12.2 Pre-Launch Checklist**

| **#** | **Check**                                                       | **Owner**     |
| ----- | --------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- |
| 1     | All 301 redirects from SIA-003 §6 live and verified             | Dev           |
| 2     | Core Web Vitals targets met on home/module/sector               | Dev           |
| 3     | WCAG 2.2 AA automated + manual checks passed                    | Dev           |
| 4     | Demo & contact forms deliver to CRM + inbox; spam protection on | Dev           |
| 5     | Analytics + consent live; conversions firing correctly          | Dev/Marketing |
| 6     | Sitemap submitted; robots correct; staging de-indexed           | Dev/SEO       |
| 7     | TLS/HSTS/security headers verified                              | Dev           |
| 8     | Legal pages published; cookie consent working                   | Marmidon      |
| 9     | Cross-browser & real-device pass complete                       | QA            |
| 10    | Backups, monitoring & alerting confirmed                        | Dev           |

**BEST PRACTICE · Launch readiness equals these gates**

The site is ready to launch when every item above is green and the PRD §12 acceptance gates are signed off.

Do not cut over the domain until the redirect map is verified - this is what protects existing SEO value.

**SECTION A**

**Appendix - NFR Traceability**

Each non-functional requirement in PRD-001 maps to a section of this specification, so the developer can prove coverage.

| **NFR** | **Requirement (summary)**        | **Implemented in** |
| ------- | -------------------------------- | ------------------ |
| NFR-01  | Performance / Core Web Vitals    | §4                 |
| NFR-02  | Scalability under traffic        | §3, §4.4           |
| NFR-03  | Mobile-first responsiveness      | §9                 |
| NFR-04  | Availability ≥ 99.9%             | §3.1               |
| NFR-05  | Security & hardening             | §5, §8.2           |
| NFR-06  | Accessibility WCAG 2.2 AA        | §10                |
| NFR-07  | SEO & discoverability            | §6                 |
| NFR-08  | Analytics & measurability        | §7                 |
| NFR-09  | Maintainability (CMS/components) | §2                 |
| NFR-10  | Privacy & compliance             | §8.2, §11          |

**SECTION B**

**Appendix - Structured Data Reference**

Indicative JSON-LD shapes the developer should adapt. Final values come from the CMS and CPM-005. (Illustrative - not literal code to paste.)

| **Type**            | **Minimum properties to populate**                        |
| ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |
| Organization        | name, url, logo, sameAs\[\], contactPoint                 |
| WebSite             | name, url, potentialAction (SearchAction)                 |
| SoftwareApplication | name, applicationCategory, description, offers (if shown) |
| BreadcrumbList      | itemListElement\[\] matching the page's breadcrumb trail  |
| BlogPosting         | headline, datePublished, author, image, articleBody       |
| FAQPage             | mainEntity\[\] of Question + acceptedAnswer               |

**NOTE · End of specification**

This specification, together with PRD-001, WDD-002, SIA-003 and CPM-005, forms the complete brief for the Marmidon website build.

**- End of Document MARM-WEB-TSR-004 -**
